edges.evidence_quote: Under LaRocca's leadership, the crime family became powerful in Pittsburgh's labor unions and established rackets in Northeast Ohio, in which it partnered with the Cleveland crime family.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family partnered with the Cleveland crime family in rackets in Northeast Ohio.

edges.evidence_quote: LaRocca also brought the Pittsburgh Mafia into an agreement with the Trafficante crime family of Tampa to manage the Sans Souci casino in Havana.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family made an agreement with the Trafficante family to manage a casino in Havana.

edges.evidence_quote: Amato began expanding his influence over the gambling rackets in and around Allegheny County, and formed a strong alliance with the Genovese crime family in New York City, who represented Pittsburgh at meetings of the Commission.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family formed a strong alliance with the Genovese family, who represented them at Commission meetings.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1960s, LaRocca's family engaged in conflict with the Cleveland crime family when they expanded into Youngstown, Ohio.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family was in conflict with the Cleveland family over expansion into Youngstown.

edges.evidence_quote: the Pennsylvania Crime Commission claimed Conley received video poker machines from a company aligned with New Jersey's Lucchese and DeCavalcante organized crime families.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family associate Conley received video poker machines from a company aligned with the Lucchese family.

edges.evidence_quote: the Pennsylvania Crime Commission claimed Conley received video poker machines from a company aligned with New Jersey's Lucchese and DeCavalcante organized crime families.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family associate Conley received video poker machines from a company aligned with the DeCavalcante family.

edges.evidence_quote: Gabriel "Kelly" Mannarino – former capo who controlled the New Kensington rackets. Mannarino had ties to the Bufalino family.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family capo Mannarino had ties to Bufalino family.

edges.evidence_quote: Pasquale "Pat" Ferruccio – a former capo who operated in Canton-Stark County, Ohio area and the Akron-Summit County, Ohio area while paying tribute to the Cleveland family.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family capo Ferruccio paid tribute to Cleveland family.

edges.evidence_quote: He established rackets in Ohio, while sharing some of the illegal income with the Cleveland crime family.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family shared illegal income with Cleveland family.

edges.evidence_quote: LaRocca also formed an agreement with Tampa crime family boss Santo Trafficante operating casinos in Havana, Cuba.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family formed agreement with Tampa family to operate casinos in Havana.

edges.evidence_quote: Frank Valenti – a former soldier; in the early 1960s he took over the Rochester family.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family soldier Valenti took over Rochester family.

edges.evidence_quote: Gesuale became the Pittsburgh Mafia's liaison between the crime family and Pagan's Motorcycle Club boss Daniel "Danny the Deacon" Zwibel.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family associate Gesuale acted as liaison with Pagans Motorcycle Club.

edges.evidence_quote: Unis was a Pittsburgh associate with ties to the Gambino family in New York.
edges.context: Pittsburgh family associate Unis had ties to Gambino family.

edges.evidence_quote: During the early 1970s the faction gained control of the gambling rackets in Youngstown and shared some of the profits with the Cleveland crime family.
edges.context: The Pittsburgh family's Youngstown faction shared gambling profits with the Cleveland crime family.
